differences.title: 1. DATA MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Flexible, supports various document structures and key-value access.
differences.rightDesc: Stores data in flexible, JSON-like documents with dynamic schemas.

differences.title: 2. QUERYING
differences.leftDesc: SQL-like N1QL for rich querying, also direct key-value access.
differences.rightDesc: Rich, object-oriented query language, powerful for document manipulation.

differences.title: 3. SCALABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Designed for high-volume transactional workloads, scales horizontally seamlessly.
differences.rightDesc: Scales horizontally via sharding, handles large datasets effectively.

differences.title: 4. CONSISTENCY
differences.leftDesc: Offers tunable consistency levels for reads and writes across replicas.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily eventual consistency, with options for stronger reads.

differences.title: 5. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Leverages in-memory caching for high-speed data access.
differences.rightDesc: Optimized for read operations, good for content-heavy apps.

differences.title: 6. USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for caching, mobile apps, and high-performance services.
differences.rightDesc: Excellent for content management systems, product catalogs, and analytics.

differences.title: 7. CLUSTERING
differences.leftDesc: Supports active-active multi-master replication for high availability.
differences.rightDesc: Uses replica sets for high availability and read scaling.
